In Kenya, a five-month old elephant bond with new families(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Neil Forster is an impatient cook who reaches for the takeaway menu rather than making food from scratch. Lorraine shows him recipes including asparagus and cream cheese puff pastry tarts, a quick way to do a roast chicken and a neat shortcut to prepare a summer fruits pudding(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers indulge in an afternoon snack as they examine the history of high tea in Britain. The pair sample a selection of tasty food and hear some fascinating stories as they embark on a quest to explore what has become a national institution(n)

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In the second episode, Liz Bonnin meets the animals doing whatever it takes to find food, joining experts to uncover the science behind their ingenious tactics and some of their unusual behaviour. Liz sees macaques using psychology to pull off a theft, tigers deceiving their prey, a wedge-billed hummingbird stealing nectar from under the beaks of its rivals, a spider conning prey with a chemical disguise, and coconut crabs who seem intent on a stealing spree(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and David Harper salvage chimney pots, vintage cases and an old washtub before they head to their next auction in Bedale, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of experts from the worlds of history, science and antiques comprising of Geoffrey Munn, Tessa Dunlop, Curtis Dowling, Simon Singh and Anita Anand sets out to triumph over the general knowledge Goliaths and win the cash prize for charity(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At the Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four family heirlooms back to life. Fine art conservator Lucia Scalisi restores a treasured painting by celebrated artist CJ Frost, while furniture restorer Will Kirk sets to work on an antique sewing table. Elsewhere, Steve Fletcher and David Burville repair a 130-year-old automaton tiger, and fountain pen restorer Laurence Oldfield tackles the restoration of a vintage Wyvern fountain pen(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. The nine remaining contestants are challenged to use their interior design skills to transform hotel bedrooms at the Chocolate Boutique Hotel in Bournemouth. The hotel has a strong chocolate theme throughout, so the competitors, split into teams of three, must incorporate dark, milk or white chocolate in their designs. At the end of the challenge, one of the contestants is sent home. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at criminal investigations. After 46-year-old Stephen Wallace is found dead in his Paisley home from a number of injuries, detectives forensically comb the crime scene for clues which will lead them to his killer(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam feels unappreciated when her daughters prefer to watch RuPaul than her own show. Comedy drama, created by and starring Pamela Adlon. Also stars Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood and Mikey Madison(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the Portuguese city of Lisbon, where he is inspired by the city's love of seafood to cook his own take on salt cod fritters(n)

programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the brigade to San Sebastian in the Basque region of Spain, where they face their toughest challenge to date competing against the kitchen staff of Michelin-starred chef Paulo Airaudo. Jason has had to bring in two new recruits to replace the chefs who have dropped out, and they struggle to find their place on the team - especially as both are used to being in charge(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Nisha Katona takes Fred Sirieix to San Sebastian for a mind-blowing meal at one of the world's top restaurants and to visit a tiny sports bar to learn the secrets of the perfect tortilla. The first place in Nisha's little black book is Mugaritz, ranked as the ninth best restaurant in the world and famous for wild experimentation - something that is made clear as soon as the menus arrive. Instead of a list of dishes to select, the Mugaritz menu is two pictures, one of a swimming fish, one of a flying fish, and Nisha and Fred make their choice, but have little clue what they are going to eat(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of teams captained by Springwatch presenter Chris Packham and zoologist Lucy Cooke across a variety of intriguing rounds. Iolo Williams and Lizzie Daly are the guests, with every correct answer wins part of a mysterious animal and as the show progresses each team builds its very own bespoke beast that they must correctly identify the parts of in the endgame, while the show is packed with breathtaking natural history clips, extraordinary animal facts and spirited debate(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: Two families from Sussex and Norfolk experience a new way of life as they swap homes - but will they love or loathe the experience?(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The genealogy detectives take on the high-value estate of a former holiday camp entertainer, while another case stalls when a woman discovers her only daughter has already died(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Student Travis Francis admits his mum makes all his meals, but now he wants to cook for her. Lorraine creates a menu inspired by his family's Caribbean roots, including a prawn, sweet potato and mango curry and tempting chocolate and rum truffles. She also demonstrates a quick way to make bread(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the history behind some of the country's most popular spices and explore how they have been used to liven up meals. The culinary duo also visit a chilli festival - with mouth-numbing consequences(n)

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: In the final episode, Liz Bonnin meets the animals going to extreme measures to survive, and reveals the some of the surprising scientific discoveries behind their ingenious behaviour. Liz examines the behaviour of cockatoos vandalising houses in Sydney, chimpanzees using bullying tactics to get to the top, crabs who hold anemones hostage to protect themselves, and the stone martens who cause millions of pounds worth of damage to cars while protecting their territories(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and David Harper search Yorkshire, from Halifax to Harrogate, picking up items including a vintage trouser press and a gunpowder flask before heading to auction(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Stars of BBC comedy Still Game take part, with Gavin Mitchell, Sanjeev Kohli, Mark Cox, Paul Young and Jane McCarry taking on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the King's Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park codebreaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered Victorian firefighter's helmet. Woodworker Will Kirk and silversmith Brenton West team up to repair a 19th-century stereoscope viewer that sparked a lifelong obsession, and soft toy rest toy restorers Amanda Middleditch and Julie Tatchell try to make a faceless old teddy bear smile again(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond's bull Major returns to work after being injured earlier in the year, but getting him to the field of cows involves a soggy detour. Raymond also travels to Switzerland with fiancee Jen to help their friends gather their valais blacknose sheep from the mountaintops. Kirsty and Aimee go sheep shopping to double their flock, while bad news forces buffalo farmer Stevie to make a shocking decision(n)

programme.title: The Octopus in My House
programme.desc: Wildlife documentary observing Professor David Scheel as he takes an octopus into his home to learn about its intelligence, and the extraordinary relationship he and his daughter develop with the creature. Named Heidi, the octopus is seen unravelling puzzles, recognising individual humans and even watching TV with the family. The film also looks at the remarkable behaviour of other octopuses around the world, from those that can change their colour and texture in a split second to the octopus that carries around its own coconut shell to hide in(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.sub-title: Dara Ó Briain, Zoe Lyons, Paul Chowdhry
programme.desc: Mock the Week host Dara O Briain comperes an evening of stand-up at London's Hammersmith Apollo, treating the audience to his own brand of humour, before introducing routines by comedy circuit stars Zoe Lyons and Paul Chowdhry(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. The nine remaining contestants are challenged to use their interior design skills to transform hotel bedrooms at Hotel Chocolate in Bournemouth. The hotel has a strong chocolate theme throughout, so the competitors, split into teams of three, must incorporate dark, milk or white chocolate in their designs. At the end of the challenge, one of the contestants is sent home. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh heads to more countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. In this first edition, he heads to Zimbabwe in the aftermath of Robert Mugabe's rule, to discover whether British people should be holidaying in the southern African country once again. He meets Chipo, a teacher who was born and raised in Harare under Mugabe's rule. She explains that although people have been coming to the country, few venture beyond Victoria Falls. She wants to show Rom what they are missing on a grand, epic road trip that takes in National Parks and close-up encounters with rhinos, elephants and the so-called 'cheetah ambassador'(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: From the ancient temples of Angkor Wat in Cambodia to the Shaolin Temple in China, discover what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the opening episode, in Cambodia at Angkor Wat, a man who believes the temples are home to his ancestral spirits risks his life to save them from the jungle, while at the Shaolin Temple in China, a Buddhist warrior monk faces a test that could change the course of his life forever(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: A couple from Cornwall who now hire out restored vintage camper vans thanks to a £5,000 ethical loan, and a would-be gardener who turns to crowdfunding to his own business(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The vets operate on Violet the koala to investigate her breathing problems, and the keepers wonder which will come off worst when a tree porcupine comes face to face with a skunk(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost dispels the confusion of the summer pruning of wisteria, and demonstrates an ingenious solution to planting in dry shade under trees. Arit Anderson is in Germany finding out about the incredible transformation of a disused industrial site into a thriving and beautiful park, beloved by visitors and the local community. Rachel de Thame heads to Gloucestershire to find out about the 100-year history of the gardens at Kiftsgate Court, and Nick Bailey profiles the many varieties of summer flowering clematis(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke, with guests Niall Strawson and Ferne Corrigan(n)

programme.title: Home Away from Home
programme.desc: A Nottingham couple and two friends from Warrington, Cheshire, sample new ways of life as they exchange homes. They then decide whether they have enjoyed the experience. Presented by Gloria Hunniford(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: While on the verge of finding heirs in line to a significant inheritance from John Clench, Michael Buerk and the Heir Hunters uncover a family secret that throws everything on its head, diverting the money in a different direction - but a family reunion makes it all worthwhile. The case of Roy Rogerson reveals a man with a passion for cricket and uncovers the fascinating but dangerous career path of his grandfather, leaving the heirs desperate to find out more(n)

programme.title: Lorraine Pascale: How to Be a Better Cook
programme.desc: Sally Mack lacks the confidence to cook for anyone except herself, but after turning 50 she decided that had to change. Lorraine creates a Chinese-inspired menu of dishes that are simple to prepare, including sweet-and-sour ribs, chicken and cashew nut stir fry and a showstopping cake(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the British tradition of cooking with food hunted from the land and prepare a selection of dishes using rabbit, grouse and venison(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Naturalist Chris Packham observes the devious tactics and sneaky tricks that some animals carry out in order to survive. New, ground-breaking science helps reveal astonishing visual trickery, from illusionist zebras to vanishing geckos and shape-shifting cuttlefish. Mischievous mimics, from snakes to baby birds disguised as caterpillars, and a lioness who looks like a male lion are also highlighted. The world's ultimate con artists can even rely on sneaky strategies against their own kind, such as the monkey that cheats its family and the lizard that fakes its death(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: New expert Angus Ashworth takes the pilgrims' watery route over the sea to the Holy Island of Lindisfarne and veteran Tripper David Harper hoofs it up the longest avenue in Scotland at Marchmont House. The duo then head towards the finishing line at an auction in Alnwick, Northumberland(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Chris Rogers, Helen Fospero, Ricky Andalcio, Miranda Krestovnikoff and Kevin Duala form another celebrity team aiming to triumph over the Eggheads. Presented by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull head to Northern Ireland, where they find out about the invention of the ejector seat, and visit Armagh Observatory, a railway museum located inside a barber shop, and SS Nomadic, known as Titanic's little sister(n)

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on specialised subjects including the Thirty Years' War, the members and music of Genesis, Shakespeare's Elizabethan tragedies, and Joss Whedon's film Serenity and Firefly TV series. They are then each afforded two minutes to demonstrate their prowess on general knowledge(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the bank holiday weekend looming, Monty Don has plenty of inspiration for gardeners from planting for late summer colour to tidying up herbs and pruning soft fruit. Nick Bailey concludes his journey into the clematis world by looking at the late summer-flowering varieties and the programme travels to Devon to meet a retired biology teacher with a passion for houseleeks(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: As the high summer of 1973 ebbs away, Bob and girlfriend Ann Reinking hire a weekend house in the country to help comfort the recently bereaved Neil Simon. Also invited are Gwen and her boyfriend Ron. Ann is concerned that Gwen won't like her, but Bob says Gwen will be on her best behaviour for Neil. Gwen proves to be in buoyant mood, excited to announce a theatre has been secured for their project of Chicago, but Bob is hesitant claiming he has a lot of work on with the Lenny script. Thus, new tensions emerge. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sindhu Vee, Rhod Gilbert and Ed Byrne join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at pathology, the causal study of disease based on the analysis of bodily fluids. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Katie Razzall presents an analysis of the day's national and international events(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights with Nish Kumar
programme.desc: Nish explores the theme 'mums and dads' at this year's Festivals in comedy, cabaret, theatre and circus. Comedian Ed Byrne explores whether he has any traits that are worth passing on to his children and Hear Word brings together 10 of Nigeria's biggest stars in a show that will shock with its frankness and amuse with its honesty(n)

programme.title: BBC Ouch
programme.desc: Chris McCausland introduces this comedy and storytelling show, featuring funny and fascinating stories told by disabled people and those with mental health difficulties. This year's theme, Lost and Found, is particularly revealing(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Anni Albers: A Life in Thread
programme.desc: The story of Anni Albers, one of the female Bauhaus pioneers who transformed the perception of textiles into modern art. In 1922, Anni enrolled at the legendary Bauhaus school of art in Weimar Germany to become a painter, but was soon moved into the weaving class which became known as the women's workshop. Here, she joined a group of women who turned the looms to their advantage and used them to create a radical new kind of modern art-textile abstraction. The film also documents Anni's later life in America, where she became one of the world's most famous living artists(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the role played by the Church during Robert the Bruce's 22-year campaign to secure Scottish independence, and how its repeated propaganda campaigns for the cause at home and abroad eventually bore fruit. The programme also examines how the 1320 Declaration of Arbroath ultimately persuaded the Pope to recognise the country as an independent nation(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus 100
programme.desc: A century after it opened its doors, this programme tells the story of the Bauhaus art school in Germany, and the men and women whose designs shaped the modern world. The programme recounts the work of Walter Gropius, architect and founder of the Bauhaus, and the teachers and students he gathered to form the influential school(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus Rules with Vic Reeves
programme.desc: Six graduates from Central St Martins are challenged to create a new work of art, design or performance inspired by artists who taught at the Bauhaus. Key figures from today's worlds of art and design - who have been influenced by the Bauhaus in their own work - set the rules for the experiment. Presented by Vic Reeves(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The comedian introduces highlights from the Edinburgh Fringe, going head to head with Scottish mindreader Colin Cloud for some probing chat, as well as getting the best and funniest festival stories from comedians Phoebe Robinson, Nick Helm, Iain Stirling and Jay Lafferty. Game of Thrones actor Daniel Portman and writer Gary McNair also feature(n)

programme.title: Jerusalem: The Making of a Holy City
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore charts the history of tension between Muslims, Christian and Jews in the city. He begins by focusing on the Mamluks' period in charge of Jerusalem following the Crusades, before exploring how life changed under the rule of the Islamic Ottoman empire. He also delves into the city's turbulent history during the 19th century, when it became prized by several European powers, and traces the origins of Jerusalem's present-day religious conflicts(n)

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the role of artists and architects in the development of American cities and culture, as the country sought to step out of Europe's shadow. He travels to Manhattan, Chicago and Massachusetts to examine the work of Norman Rockwell, John Sloan, George Bellows and Louis Sullivan, before charting the links between the Great Depression of the 1920s and 30s and the rise of abstract expressionism. He also visits Jackson Pollock's studio in Long Island and Mark Rothko's Chapel in Houston, Texas(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the role played by the Church during Robert the Bruce's 22-year campaign to secure Scottish independence, and how its repeated propaganda campaigns for the cause at home and abroad eventually bore fruit. The programme also examines how the 1320 Declaration of Arbroath ultimately persuaded the Pope to recognise the country as an independent nation(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus 100
programme.desc: A century after it opened its doors, this programme tells the story of the Bauhaus art school in Germany, and the men and women whose designs shaped the modern world. The programme recounts the work of Walter Gropius, architect and founder of the Bauhaus, and the teachers and students he gathered to form the influential school(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Gary Davies introduce the edition first aired on May 19, featuring Prefab Sprout, Kylie Minogue, Derek B, Aztec Camera, Heart, Ofra Haza, Hothouse Flowers, Debbie Gibson, Belinda Carlisle, Billy Bragg with Cara Tivey, Wet Wet Wet and Liverpool FC(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: Racing champion Martin, who is a member of Britain's oldest flying club The Royal Aero Club, prepares to compete for the Schneider Trophy, in which 14 pilots will fly around outlying islands in Alderney in the Channel Islands. Elsewhere, a cargo pilot contends with a tight schedule, and must cross the Irish sea even during bad weather conditions, while the team at DHL's warehouse processes a hundred and fifty thousand parcels each night. Plus, at NATS, bad weather grounds planes and dangerous 'wind sheer' causes pilots to abort landings(n)

programme.title: Operation Wild
programme.desc: Clare Balding travels to Japan where she visits inventor and vet Dr Keiichi Ueda. The vet has dedicated the past 12 years of his career to helping a dolphin called Fuji who has no tail as a result of a bacterial infection and he is hoping that a new prosthetic tail will enable her to swim properly again. Steve Leonard heads to the rainforest of Laos, where Edinburgh Zoo vet Romain Pizzi is undertaking keyhole surgery on an endangered moon bear called Champa who suffers from excess fluid on her brain. Steve then visits Florida where reptile specialist Doug Mader is using the latest technology to save Martha, an alligator with a badly blocked gut. Clare and Steve also follow the stories of a Galapagos tortoise with a mystery illness, a five-ton elephant suffering from an infected tusk and a tiger that keeps having nose bleeds(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Helen Czerski explores the science of heat, and how the ability to harness it has led to some of humanity's greatest innovations, from steam engines to plasmas(n)

programme.title: Immortal Egypt with Joann Fletcher
programme.desc: Joann explores the peak of ancient Egyptian civilisation by looking at the Colossi of Memnon, two massive stone statues depicting Pharaoh Amenhotep III, and examining the lives of the workers and artisans involved in the building of the Valley of the Kings. This golden age was threatened by the growing power of Karnak's priests, and Joann reveals how Tutankhamen's early death was a chance for Egypt to start afresh and rewrite history, but was undermined by decades of state-sanctioned looting of the tombs(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Gary Davies introduce the edition first aired on May 19, featuring Prefab Sprout, Kylie Minogue, Derek B, Aztec Camera, Heart, Ofra Haza, Hothouse Flowers, Debbie Gibson, Belinda Carlisle, Billy Bragg with Cara Tivey, Wet Wet Wet and Liverpool FC(n)

programme.title: Anni Albers: A Life in Thread
programme.desc: The story of Anni Albers, one of the female Bauhaus pioneers who transformed the perception of textiles into modern art. In 1922, Anni enrolled at the legendary Bauhaus school of art in Weimar Germany to become a painter, but was soon moved into the weaving class which became known as the women's workshop. Here, she joined a group of women who turned the looms to their advantage and used them to create a radical new kind of modern art-textile abstraction. The film also documents Anni's later life in America, where she became one of the world's most famous living artists(n)

programme.title: Art of America
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his journey through American art by examining how it reflected and challenged the country's post-war culture. He begins in the purpose-built suburb of Levittown, Pennsylvania, before travelling to New York to examine works by Jasper Johns and Andy Warhol, then revealing how the otherworldly qualities of Los Angeles inspired painter Ed Ruscha. The presenter also discusses his love for the work of Philip Guston, meets Warhol's former lover Billy Name and artists Jeff Koons and James Rosenquist, and assesses artistic responses to the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks(n)

programme.title: Bauhaus Rules with Vic Reeves
programme.desc: Six graduates from Central St Martins are challenged to create a new work of art, design or performance inspired by artists who taught at the Bauhaus. Key figures from today's worlds of art and design - who have been influenced by the Bauhaus in their own work - set the rules for the experiment. Presented by Vic Reeves(n)

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Helen Czerski explores the science of heat, and how the ability to harness it has led to some of humanity's greatest innovations, from steam engines to plasmas(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.sub-title: BBC Proms: Rattle Conducts Belshazzar's Feast
programme.desc: At the Royal Albert Hall, Walton's choral masterpiece Belshazzar's Feast gets the Proms treatment with conductor Simon Rattle, the London Symphony Orchestra, the LSO Chorus, the Orfeo Catala Choir and Orfeo Catala Youth Choir and Canadian baritone Gerald Finley as soloist. Plus, Varese's celebration of the modern city in sound Amériques and Charles Koechlin's Jungle Book-inspired Les bandar-log. Presented by Suzy Klein(n)

programme.title: Smashing Hits! The 80s Pop Map of Britain and Ireland
programme.desc: Midge Ure, lead singer of Ultravox and one of the men behind Band Aid, and Kim Appleby, who had a string of hits with her sister Mel in the Stock, Aitken & Waterman-produced band Mel and Kim, revisit the 1980s to determine why certain cities produced their own diverse tunes. In the first episode, they explore London's New Romantic movement, travel to Coventry to investigate the rise of Ska and speak to some of Sheffield's electronic music pioneers(n)

programme.title: Snow Patrol - Ward Park 3
programme.desc: Snow Patrol perform a homecoming gig at Ward Park in Bangor, Co Down, as the band go on a journey through their early classics to songs from their most recent album. Celebrating 25 years in music, the rockers will be treating the crowd to hits from their breakthrough release Final Straw and its follow-up Eyes Open, and last year's album Wildness(n)

programme.title: Hits, Hype & Hustle: An Insider's Guide to the Music Business
programme.desc: Music promoter John Giddings tells the story of how live performance has become a billion-pound industry. Where once bands would tour just to promote an album, in the age of downloads and dwindling record sales, the live arena is booming. The programme also tells the story of the original Isle of Wight festival, where a bunch of young promoters with big ideas persuaded Bob Dylan, the Who and Leonard Cohen to perform. The event transformed the music industry, and, for the young John who was in the audience, inspired a whole career. Contributors include Phil Collins, Stewart Copeland, Mel C and Alex James(n)

programme.title: Arthur

programme.desc: Documentary paying tribute to sporting heroes from the past, beginning with Chris Hallam, the wheelchair racer from south Wales who shone a light on Paralympic sport(n)

programme.title: Heartbreak Holiday
programme.desc: Maxine opens up about her dating issues to Archie, before shocking the group with her story. A big night out sees Erin move on from Jordy, while Luke struggles with his commitment issues again(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Canadian comedian Katherine Ryan is the latest celebrity to dig into her family's past in the popular genealogy documentary. Her investigation leads her to a Methodist minister and his family in colonial Nova Scotia, cod traders in Newfoundland, and eventually to her English roots in Dorset(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle observe events as rare Amur tiger Yana goes into labour, meaning the Park's first cubs for nearly two decades are imminent. Kate also catches up on life in the gorilla house with the focus on silverback Kesho, while Jean Johansson discovers why coconuts are just the job for the dental health of Cape Porcupines(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore two flats in Seaham in Durham, a former bank in Westhoughton near Manchester and a building with a shop and residential space in Surbiton in Surrey. All three are sold under the hammer and their new owners explain their ambitious plans to add value to their acquisitions(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt
programme.desc: In Bridge of Allan, Karen and her 75-year-old father go into business together combining the ancient craft of glassblowing and Scotland's famous tipple, creating an unique range of whisky gifts. With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to northern Lincolnshire to meet the volunteers from Immingham in Bloom. They've been taking part in Britain In Bloom for seven years and have won gold before. However, this time the group is upping the ante by taking a huge risk. Chris finds out how they plan to retain their crown by including the port itself in their entry, with plans to brighten this enormous grey industrial area with colourful flowers(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: The reds and blues go bargain hunting in Lincolnshire, with the help of experts Nick Hall and Gary Pe. Plus, presenter Charlie Ross learns about learns about the rules on finding treasure at the bottom of the garden(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win a £10,000 jackpot, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the Welsh Borders with a retired couple of keen gardeners who have £500,000 to plough into buying a peaceful home in the country. While in the area, Nicki meets one of the very few weathervane makers left in the UK and while trying her hand at creating one, she learns why for centuries, it has been such a striking symbol gracing the tops of churches and manor houses across the countryside(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the 1937 case of 23-year-old Ruby Keen, who was found strangled to death with her own scarf in an alleyway in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire. Ex-boyfriend Leslie Stone was found guilty of murder and hanged. More than 80 years later, Stone's nieces cannot believe their uncle committed such a heinous crime and want to learn more about his character and the case, which was kept a secret from them by their mother(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Ore Oduba present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Kate Humble: Off the Beaten Track
programme.desc: Return of the programme in which Kate and her Welsh sheepdog Teg explore Wales' countryside. She begins in North Wales, trying to climb the tallest man-made structure in the country to fix some loose cables on the Arfon Transmission Tower. Next, Kate learns how to overcome natural obstacles with the novel activity of packrafting - using a lightweight inflatable canoe. On the north-east coast, Kate meets a pack of dogs trained to carry out wildlife conservation by sniffing out endangered species, then on the north-west coast she digs and prepares a grave for a new resident who in death wants to give himself to help nature thrive(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Mel demands an explanation from the police about how Hunter escaped - and insists they find her son safely. Rainie and Max argue about their arrangements over Abi. However, things only get worse when Ian and Kathy get involved - prompting Bobby to intervene. Meanwhile, Stuart tells Rainie he is worried about her continued use of painkillers(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: The first part of a two-legged edition of the quiz, which sees former England striker Darren Bent, world champion 110m hurdler Colin Jackson, world Taekwondo title-winner Bianca Walkden and Olympic heptathlon medallist Kelly Sotherton join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: Edith and Phil discover a bookshop hosting local author Wendy Stevens, conducting a signing of her inaugural book, The Lemming Family. However, Phil is later outraged to find out that the characters are thinly disguised portraits of himself and the family. Roger's hopes of becoming a Social Services-supported carer look like being revived, though not quite in the way he imagines when the infamous Mrs Pool makes a reappearance. Comedy, starring Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese and Sue Johnston(n)

credits.actor: Alison Steadman, Jason Watkins, John Cleese, Sue Johnston

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: The Browns are excited as preparations for Rory and Dino's big day are in full swing - although Agnes's mood is dampened when she meets flamboyant celebrity wedding planner La La Doggy. The foul-mouthed matriarch also clashes with Father Damien when he raises religious concerns about the ceremony - and to top it all off, she gets more than she bargained for when she invites Cathy's boyfriend's parents over for the evening. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll, with Jennifer Gibney, Rory Cowan and Conor Moloney(n)

credits.actor: Brendan O'Carroll, Jennifer Gibney, Rory Cowan, Conor Moloney

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen references clips from half a century and more of Welsh television as he sets out to discover the truth about love. It's a story of lonely hearts, stag nights and kissogram nuns, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sasha is forced to work with her ex-husband Ned Hancock when their teams investigate the death of film critic Oliver Houghton, whose body was discovered floating in the Thames. UCOS delves into the victim's past and explores his link to the murder of a conceptual artist 20 years previously, while Hancock's knowledge of London's hidden past proves invaluable when a piece of occult footage from the 1970s plunges the investigators into the macabre world of human sacrifice. Tamzin Outhwaite and Denis Lawson star, with a guest appearance by Barnaby Kay(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate the fake Botox treatment that left one woman with life changing injuries. Elsewhere, a report on false online reviews that are ruining business across the UK, deadly fake camping stoves that can explode when they are lit, and Michelle tests imitation Airpods headphones(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern travel to Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, to meet Antonietta Tulino and her three children, hoping to help the family change their spending habits. In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.desc: Mike and Michael are forced to call the police when a confrontation at a Yorkshire car dealership turns ugly. Can they get the man who was sold a faulty car the money he is owed? In an effort to confront their money-spending ways, Alex and Steph resort to shock tactics, and set up a pop-up-shop in the family home displaying all of their biggest spends, with shocking results. Can Alex and Steph help this family start saving, rather than spending?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: A Buddhist painter battles the elements at Boudhanath Stupa in Nepal to honour Buddha's birthday, and in Iraq, at Lalish Temple, a young Yazedi woman goes in search of comfort after being traumatised by the recent conflict. At the Holy Sepulchre in Jerusalem - where a miracle is believed to happen at the same place, at the same time every year - an orthodox Christian delivers a holy flame into the hands of a bishop. Documentary showcasing what people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth.(n)

programme.desc: Mike and Michael are forced to call the police when a confrontation at a Yorkshire car dealership turns ugly. Can they get the man who was sold a faulty car the money he is owed? Rob and Gerald are called to remove a group of squatters from a family business. The unwelcome guests have racked up thousands of pounds in utility bills leaving the owner out of pocket and the place a mess. Tommy goes in search of a debtor on the beautiful Welsh coast and it seems a boat removal will be the most likely outcome(n)

programme.title: A Matter of Life and Debt

With the aid of a £25,000 loan the business is continuing to keep a dying art alive. Plus, a transgender entrepreneur takes control of her future after struggling with employment(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom

However, this time the group is upping the ante by taking a huge risk. Chris finds out how they plan to retain their crown by including the port itself in their entry, with plans to brighten this enormous grey industrial area with colourful flowers(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate the 1937 case of 23-year-old Ruby Keen, who was found strangled to death with her own scarf in an alleyway in Leighton Buzzard, Bedfordshire. Ex-boyfriend Leslie Stone was found guilty of murder and hanged. More than 80 years later, Stone's nieces cannot believe their uncle committed such a heinous crime and want to learn more about his character and the case, which was kept a secret from them by their mother(n)

Roger's hopes of becoming a Social Services-supported carer look like being revived, though not quite in the way he imagines when the infamous Mrs Pool makes a reappearance. programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys

programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Cameras follow Ross Senior and Ross Junior, a father and son serving time on the same wing. programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emma tells her mum Fiona about Steve being her biological dad, and Ed agrees to let James lend him £5,000, but later Michael finds Ed sat staring at a gambling site. And as Geoff sees his doctor, it is clear there is nothing wrong with him(n)

programme.title: Deep Water
programme.desc: Lisa tries to make amends for her part in Lucinda's disappearance but is starting to see a different side to Kate and Guy. Her husband Joe tells her that she should focus on her own family, as their daughter Sally has struggles to cope with the recent events. Guy learns that Sally has been keeping information from the investigation and storms round to Lisa's home. Meanwhile, Detective Constable Joanne Aspinall starts to look into the Rivertys' family history, and grows suspicious of Guy. Starring Anna Friel, Sinead Keenan and Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: In a first for the programme, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell present an appeal for information on behalf of two families whose loved ones have been reported missing to the police. They look at the cases of Matthew Bone, who disappeared in March 2018, and Simon Greaves, who went missing five years ago. They also follow the story of Beryl Everall, who last saw her mother more than 60 years ago when she mysteriously disappeared from her life. All contact with her mother's side of the family was lost, and she was never spoken of again(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emma announces she is leaving Weatherfield, and Tim tells Eileen to pull herself together. Meanwhile, Bethany informs Daniel that she's going to write up her experience with Nathan as it might help other girls to avoid the same trap(n)

programme.title: Rescue - River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: Ireland's national champion surfer Gearoid McDaid takes a serious fall in the surf and badly injures his leg, so members of the Irish Tow Surf Rescue Club are asked to pick him up and lead him to safety. The Kinder Mountain Rescue Team is called to a mountain biker who's fallen over the handlebars and torn an artery. His life is in the balance and it is only the quick thinking of a passer-by and the prompt arrival of the rescue team and air ambulance that ensures his survival(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Amy and Tracy set off to try to find Emma, Shona is furious at Max for ruining Lily's party, and Eileen returns to work and announces she's giving up men. Elsewhere, Ed and Aggie are reunited, much to the delight of their sons(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh and Cole investigate the robbery of a truck filled with fine art. Following a new development in Natalie's life, Cole deals with lingering feelings for her. Meanwhile, Riana blackmails her father into letting her go to a concert after she catches him in a lie that he told Trish. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott, with Chandler Kinney(n)

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove(n)

programme.desc: A sniper kills the people he blames for his father's death. An accountant working for one of the victims sympathises with the gunman, and helps him to track down his next target(n)

programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 is assigned to protect an Middle Eastern diplomat who is being pursued by assassins. programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.desc: Greg Haines presents action from both races from Cadwell Park, where the eighth round of the season took place. With commentary by Jack Burnicle and James Whitham(n)

programme.title: Speed Freaks
programme.desc: Zef Eisenberg explores performance-enhancing technology in a state-of-the-art electric test vehicle, and work continues on his Porsche's braking and cooling systems. He also sets the Speed Freaks the challenge of converting an old Turbo Diesel Mercedes into an electric car(n)

programme.title: Dirty Harry
programme.desc: Maverick San Francisco detective 'Dirty' Harry Callahan sets out to capture a deranged sniper picking off victims at random. Although his own tactics barely stay within the confines of the law, he is determined the killer will face his particular brand of justice, regardless of his superiors' disapproval. Don Siegel's thriller, starring Clint Eastwood, Harry Guardino and Andy Robinson(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits South America in search of a predator believed to be more fearsome than a piranha. Tipped off about an attack on a diver by a single fish, he knows there is a real monster to find, but lightning storms threaten his journey as he heads deeper into the remote jungles of Suriname(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade enlists the supernatural help of a local witch doctor to catch the notorious goliath tigerfish in the Congo River in Central Africa. The creature has the biggest teeth of any freshwater species, and combines the strength, speed and ferocity of all the monsters Jeremy has encountered up to now, pushing him to his physical and mental limits(n)

programme.title: Fishing Allstars
programme.desc: Dean Macey and Adam Rooney are joined by England international angler Matt Godfrey in the Cotswolds, where they target 5lb tench at an idyllic lake, before heading to the Gloucester canal late at night in search of a 10lb zander, otherwise known as 'the vampire fish'(n)

programme.title: GoldenEye
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale(n)

programme.title: World Series of Darts: New Zealand
programme.desc: A chance to see day one of the inaugural event from Claudelands Arena in Hamilton, featuring all eight first-round matches, played over the best of 11 legs. Michael van Gerwen, Rob Cross, Gary Anderson, Daryl Gurney, Peter Wright, James Wade, Simon Whitlock and Raymond van Barneveld were the eight seeded PDC representatives who would pit themselves against eight leading players from Oceania - wildcards Kyle Anderson, Corey Cadby and Cody Harris, and qualifiers Haupai Puha, Craig Cadwell, Damon Heta, Ben Robb and David Platt, with a first prize of £20,000 up for grabs. With commentary by Stuart Pyke and Dan Dawson(n)

programme.title: American History X
programme.desc: A neo-Nazi is thrown into prison for murder and gradually comes to realise the error of his ways. On his release, he discovers his younger brother is heading down the same extremist path, and tries to stop the teenager from repeating his mistakes. Drama, starring Edward Norton, Edward Furlong, Avery Brooks, Fairuza Balk and Stacy Keach(n)

programme.title: Age Gap Love
programme.sub-title: Cradle-Snatching OAPs: Big Age Gap Love
programme.desc: Documentary following couples from across the UK and the US who are determined to prove that mileage on the clock is no barrier to real romance - despite suffering judgement and backlash from friends and family. Norma and Chris from Brighton have been together since meeting at Butlin's when Chris was 18. Despite their 32-year age gap and having to struggle with Norma's ill health, they have remained together through good and bad. In Florida, Vietnam veteran Joe and aspiring artist Angela, 22, are out to show that love conquers all despite the critics, and the 61-year-old has even written a love song to lay his feelings bare. Elsewhere, Mandy, 56, is still hunting for 'the one' but insists on dating men in their twenties, while Michael is hoping to help his wife, 27-year-old Tanisha from Sierra Leone, pursue a career as a model after she receives UK citizenship(n)

programme.desc: Documentary meeting patients in search of answers for their mysterious conditions. Twelve-year-old Cameron was a healthy, high-achieving pupil - but that all changed overnight when he woke up with excruciating head pains, uncontrollable body tics and intense hallucinations. Unable to get an answer in the UK, he and his family now travel to the US in search of a lasting cure. programme.title: Gotham
programme.desc: Criminal mastermind Theo Galavan is riding a wave of public adulation following the bloody events of the charity gala, but Jerome's actions have caused countless copycats to take inspiration from his brand of gleeful insanity. In response, Captain Nathaniel Barnes (Michael Chiklis) is drafted in as the new leader of GCPD's task force. As a man of action with high morals and a disdain for corruption, Gordon sees a kindred spirit in Barnes, and the pair hit it off. However, should Barnes decide to take down Cobblepot, Jim worries his newfound ally might discover he himself is guilty of colluding with Gotham's criminals. Meanwhile, forensic scientist Edward Nygma works up the courage to ask his long-time crush Kristin Kringle out on a date, and things seem to be going well - until that strange voice in Nygma's head begins to speak up. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Eileen Finn invites her fellow contestants to the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumberland, for the second round of the contest, but struggles to follow the impressive first event. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at the Cavendish in Bournemouth, run by Tim and Karen Sturgess. programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

However, despite a generous £500,000 budget, the couple discover they can't have everything they want(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Isaac and Renee are at a crossroads with their little house in the suburb of Yarrawarrah, south of Sydney. Retired rugby league player Isaac believes that his family of four needs a new dwelling with more space as the children grow, but executive assistant Renee thinks that, with some internal adjustments, their current home will suit their needs. Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker help the duo determine whether to invest where they are or move elsewhere(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Documentary capturing the behaviour of the animals at Chester Zoo. An outbreak of disease in the penguin colony leaves chick Munch fighting for his life. Sulawesi macaque Lisa gives birth and dad Mamassa is providing pretty useless, so older brother Beaker steps in to help with the babysitting. Andean spectacled bear Bernie is determined to woo Lima when she enters her short window of fertility, and young male tortoise Football muscles in on 71-year-old Bert's harem of females(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats a Persian cat that has been hit by a car, requiring treatments involving a skin graft and putting its crushed bones back together. programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: In the aftermath of a violent confrontation, Claire follows her conscience as a surgeon, even though it could put her life at risk. Fantasy adventure, starring Caitriona Balfe(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 21-year-old woman on her first trip to London from Northern Ireland appears to be experiencing kidney failure, and medics fear she could be at risk of a cardiac arrest. programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer and Kirstie Allsopp are in south-east London with two couples for whom timing is critical. In and around the Isle of Dogs and Greenwich, Lia and Andrew have saved a big deposit and have a sizeable budget of £600,000 to spend. But, after witnessing the crash of 2008, Andrew is nervous of spending his hard-earned money for fear the market will dip. Lucy and Dan live in a B&B and if they don't find somewhere soon their vintage campervan will become their home. Kirstie shows the couple two potential properties, but the third option creates a reaction she isn't expecting and suddenly the search is thrown up in the air(n)

Andrew Winter's job is to find a new house within budget that has the extras to lure Nat away from Team Love It(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Four Sumatran orang-utans escape from their quarters, and tempting them back inside proves easier said than done, while the dominant female meerkat faces trouble from her daughter, who is launching physical attacks upon her mother. programme.desc: The team visits Belton House stately home near Grantham, Lincolnshire, to investigate the creation of the Machine Gun Corps during the First World War. The regiment was set up in the grounds, but today there is virtually no sign of its existence and most regimental records have been destroyed. programme.desc: Documentary exploring the story of King George V's youngest child, unravelling some of the mysteries and misconceptions surrounding him. Here, new research and photographic evidence are combined to build up a picture of his life(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team explore a site at Brancaster in Norfolk that is believed to have been a Roman 'shore fort', and possibly a key military outpost. They amass 2,500 finds - their biggest-ever haul - which provide information on contemporary decorative arts, sport and much more, and also undertake a high-definition geophysics survey that generates enough data to crash the computers. The biggest surprise reveals evidence of a high level of trade with the local population, and clues as to how Britons living near the site benefited financially from the Roman presence(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate an Iron Age hill fort in Cardiff that dominates a city housing estate, and explore whether the site could be the long-lost ancient capital of south Wales. With five acres of ground and half a mile of ramparts and ditches to examine, time is not on their side, but, helped by the local community, finds such as the remains of jewellery and drinking vessels help paint a vivid picture of domestic life around 800BC(n)

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: The Naked Chef Bares All

programme.title: Caught on Camera
programme.sub-title: Flights from Hell: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: With passenger numbers sky rocketing, airports and planes are increasingly becoming pressurised environments. Footage captured by people who were there highlights some of the most dramatic, extraordinary and unusual moments that UK passengers have experienced this year. From terrible behaviour in the terminal and tantrums on the tarmac to full-scale brawls on board, the programme hears from the people who were involved. Expert analysis from industry insiders and psychologists explains why people behave how they do on flights from hell(n)

programme.title: Jade: The Reality Star Who Changed Britain
programme.desc: Ten years on from her death, this series explores the rollercoaster life of Jade Goody, one of the most loved - and hated - reality TV stars, to tell a bigger story of class, politics and cultural change in Britain, at a time when reality TV became the biggest and most polarising form of entertainment in the country. programme.desc: The concluding programme meets a new breed of trader - the British men and women who play the financial markets online from their kitchens, sofas and bedrooms. Mother-of-three Jane squeezes currency trading in-between the school run and her work as a nurse, and is hoping to raise capital by selling her 10 Bengal kittens. Justyn and Akil are both convinced that trading will be their ticket to a lavish lifestyle, while Rene gave up a successful career as an antiques dealer for a new existence as a trader, and is undeterred despite seven years of struggling to break even. Saskia Reeves narrates(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The 1,000th episode of the quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman, who switched roles for the day to mark the milestone episode(n)
